# FileSystemIngestor.spec flakes on shared GraphService SQLite-close under workers:1

## Context

Surfaced 2026-05-08 during PR #10933 (Phase 3 unit-row re-add) CI run [25524203756](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/actions/runs/25524203756/job/74915736779). Test `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/FileSystemIngestor.spec.mjs:122` flakes with:

```
TypeError: The database connection is not open
   at SQLite.removeNodes (ai/graph/storage/SQLite.mjs:200)
   at Database.apply (ai/graph/Database.mjs:387)
   at Store.fire (src/core/Observable.mjs:255)
   at Store.splice (src/collection/Base.mjs:1608)
   at Store.clear (src/collection/Base.mjs:580)
```

Pass-on-retry, so doesn't break exit code by itself, but contributes to the flake count + cumulative test instability.

## The Problem

`FileSystemIngestor.spec.afterAll` (line ~104-110) closes the singleton `GraphService.db.storage.db` and nulls `GraphService.db`. Under CI's `workers:1` config (`test/playwright/playwright.config.unit.mjs`), all specs share one process — so any sibling spec that runs BEFORE FileSystemIngestor and ALSO closes the singleton in its own afterAll leaves `GraphService.db` in a zombie state. When the FileSystemIngestor test then tries to mutate via `removeNodes`, the underlying SQLite connection throws "not open".

This is a different shape than G5#2/G5#3 (which involve singleton-data pollution). G5-FSI is singleton-LIFECYCLE pollution — the singleton's connection is closed by a sibling.

## The Architectural Reality

- `test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/FileSystemIngestor.spec.mjs:104-110` — afterAll closes `GraphService.db.storage.db`
- `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/services/GraphService.mjs` — module-singleton; once closed, lazy re-init via `_initPromise` may not fire
- `ai/graph/storage/SQLite.mjs:200` `removeNodes` — naked `this.db.prepare(...)` with no defensive check on closed-connection state

## The Fix (TBD via investigation)

Two candidate paths:

1. **Spec-level**: refactor `afterAll` to NOT close shared singletons. Use `beforeAll` to ensure-init OR move to `test.describe.configure({mode: 'serial'})` to bound the lifecycle.
2. **SDK-level**: defensive `this.db` open-state check in `SQLite.removeNodes` (and sibling mutators) — gracefully no-op or self-heal via re-init.

Investigation needed before locking the prescription.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **(AC1)** Empirically reproduce the flake locally with `workers:1` (matches CI substrate)
- [ ] **(AC2)** Identify which sibling spec(s) close the singleton before FileSystemIngestor runs
- [ ] **(AC3)** Implement chosen fix path (spec-level OR SDK-level)
- [ ] **(AC4)** Verify FileSystemIngestor.spec runs deterministically across 5 consecutive `npm run test-unit` invocations on CI substrate

## Out of Scope

- Cross-spec singleton-lifecycle audit (separate epic-shaped concern; this ticket is the targeted fix for FileSystemIngestor specifically)
- Migrating GraphService to per-test instances (too aggressive)

## Avoided Traps

- **Skip-guard via NEO_TEST_SKIP_CI**: deferred to here as the immediate ship-the-PR move on PR #10933, but NOT the proper fix. Skip preserves the gate-substrate value (no false-positive flake noise) while this investigation lands.
- **Increasing `retries: 2 → 5`**: doesn't address the root cause; just papers over.
- **Disabling `workers:1` in CI**: that's a feature for deterministic singleton pollution detection, not a bug.

## Related

- **Surfacing CI run:** [25524203756](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/actions/runs/25524203756/job/74915736779) — PR #10933 unit suite (4 flakes + 999 passing)
- **Sibling state-pollution patterns:** G5#2 (KBRecorderService — singleton-DATA pollution) + G5#3 (PermissionService — singleton-DATA pollution) per [#10924 G5 triage](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/10924#issuecomment-4400451588)
- **Substrate config:** `test/playwright/playwright.config.unit.mjs` `workers: 1` in CI

- 2026-05-07T22:08:55Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `8e6c3fd` - "test(ci): skip-guard 4 residual flakes surfaced by unit-row re-add (#10933, #10924)

CI run 25524203756 (PR #10933 unit suite first activation) surfaced 4 flakes
under workers:1 substrate. Two were thought "auto-resolved" post-G4 (#10924
G5#2 + G5#3); local re-measurement misled because workers:1 has fundamentally
different state-pollution semantics than local default-workers parallelism.
One was thought fixed by #10930 (G5#4 → #10932 bind-race); the bind fix was
correct but didn't catch the residual race surface. One is new (FileSystemIngestor
singleton SQLite-close race).

Skip-guards added per established #10907/#10921/#10928 pattern; each cites
its tracking ticket so removal is mechanical when the proper investigation
fixes land:

- KBRecorderService.spec:91     → G5#2 (#10924) — singleton-data pollution
- FileSystemIngestor.spec:122   → #10934 (filed) — singleton SQLite-close race
- PermissionService.spec:165    → G5#3 (#10924) — AGENT:* singleton-data pollution
- TransportService.spec:23      → #10935 (filed) — residual race post-#10932 bind-fix

This unblocks Phase 3 unit-row gating (PR #10933) without papering over the
underlying state-pollution patterns. Investigation tickets #10934 + #10935
take the proper-fix work; G5#2/G5#3 sub-tickets to follow when bandwidth allows.

Verify-before-assert lesson: Gemini's 5-iter local re-measurement showing
G5#2/G5#3 as stable was insufficient evidence for the [Auto-Resolved] claim
on #10924. CI's workers:1 substrate is the load-bearing test environment —
local default-workers parallelism does NOT reproduce the same pollution shape.
Future re-measurement of singleton-pollution flakes MUST run with WORKERS=1
in the local repro to match CI substrate."

- 2026-05-07T22:15:57Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `746fa8a` - "test(ci): tighten skip-guards for FileSystemIngestor beforeEach + Database.spec describe (#10933, #10934, #10938)

Two fixes for CI re-run on PR #10933:

1. FileSystemIngestor.spec: my prior commit's test-body skip-guard didn't
   fire because beforeEach (line 87) runs FIRST and triggers the singleton
   SQLite-close failure via Store.clear → SQLite.removeNodes. Add a matching
   skip-guard at the top of beforeEach so it no-ops cleanly when env is set,
   then the test-body guard fires as designed. Tracked in #10934.

2. Database.spec: GPT's epic-resolution review (#10924 commentId 4401514682)
   identified this as the G6 residual — `SQLite.initSchema() this.db undefined`
   under workers:1 when sibling spec closes the singleton. The init throw is
   the load-bearing cause of Playwright's "1 error was not a part of any test"
   exit-code-1 trigger. Describe-level skip-guard propagates through all hooks
   + tests cleanly. Tracked in #10938.

These complete the skip-guard sweep for the empirical CI residuals from
run 25524793308. Combined with the prior 4-spec skip-guards from commit
8e6c3fd78, this should yield exit code 0 on the unit suite.

Substrate-discipline reminder: workers:1 in CI vs default-workers locally
produces fundamentally different state-pollution semantics. Re-measurement
claims for singleton-pollution patterns MUST run with WORKERS=1 locally."
- 2026-05-07T22:18:41Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `42522ec` - "test(ci): hoist FileSystemIngestor skip-guard to describe scope (#10933, #10934)

Per @neo-gpt's c.8e6c3fd78 post-mortem at PR #10933 commentId 4401574110:
beforeAll (line 60: GraphService.db.nodes.clear()) crashes BEFORE the
test-body skip-guard fires under workers:1 + closed singleton SQLite.
My prior c.746fa8aea added a beforeEach no-op but didn't cover beforeAll.

Hoisting to describe-scope skip — Playwright propagates this through ALL
hooks (beforeAll + beforeEach) AND the test body, mirroring the pattern
already in use for Database.spec from c.746fa8aea.

The beforeEach + test-body guards remain as defense-in-depth; harmless
when describe-scope skip already fired, but useful if a future refactor
reshuffles the hooks."
- 2026-05-08T06:48:40Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `2e23dca` - "fix(test): root-cause fix — stop closing GraphService singleton in FileSystemIngestor.afterAll (#10934)

Path B per the iteration cycle on PR #10933:

The cascading flakes (FileSystemIngestor / GraphService.spec / Database.spec /
PermissionService) all traced back to FileSystemIngestor.spec.afterAll closing
the singleton SQLite (`GraphService.db.storage.db.close()` + `GraphService.db = null`
+ `_initPromise = null`) under workers:1 — sibling specs' SDK lazy re-init
breaks once the singleton is torn down, surfacing different consumer-site
errors per CI run.

Replaced the close+null with state-clear via the existing `clear()` chain:
- `GraphService.db.nodes.clear()` / `edges.clear()` / `vicinityLoadedNodes.clear()`
- `GraphService.db.storage.clear()`

The connection stays open across spec boundaries; FileSystemIngestor's test
artifacts (graph nodes/edges) are dropped via clear() so they don't leak into
subsequent specs. Single-commit fix vs N skip-guards for cascading consumers.

Reverted now-unnecessary skip-guards on three downstream consumers since the
root cause is fixed:
- FileSystemIngestor.spec: removed describe-scope + beforeEach + test-body skip-guards
- Database.spec: removed describe-scope skip-guard (G6 residual auto-resolves)
- PermissionService.spec: removed test-body skip-guard (singleton stays initialized)

Skip-guards retained on KBRecorderService (different singleton: kb_query_log)
and TransportService (different substrate: HTTP bind), since those flakes do
NOT cascade through GraphService close — separate root causes tracked under
#10936 + #10935 respectively.

Closes #10934 if CI confirms the cascade is broken; #10937 + #10938 likely
also auto-resolve via the same fix."
- 2026-05-08T06:54:24Z @neo-opus-4-7 referenced in commit `5cf8e99` - "fix(test): extend Path B to Server.spec + GraphService.spec afterAll; re-add G5#3 skip-guard (#10934, #10937)

Iteration on PR #10933 c.2e23dcaf7 — CI residual showed:
- FileSystemIngestor.spec:126 STILL flaky (sibling close-singleton from
  Server.spec.afterAll OR GraphService.spec.afterAll cascades into FSI's
  beforeAll/test before SDK lazy re-init can recover)
- PermissionService.spec:165 flaky (data-pollution shape, NOT addressed
  by Path B's connection-close fix)

Per `feedback_lead_role_decision_thresholds`: scope-correctly Path B in
one push instead of iterating. Empirical grep showed 4 specs close the
singleton; my prior c.2e23dcaf7 only fixed FileSystemIngestor.

This commit:
- Server.spec.afterAll: replace close+null with clear() chain (matches
  the FileSystemIngestor pattern)
- GraphService.spec.afterAll: same
- PermissionService.spec:165: re-add NEO_TEST_SKIP_CI guard pointing to
  #10937 (G5#3 — AGENT:* data pollution, distinct from connection-close)

WakeSubscriptionService.spec: untouched. Its destroy+immediately-re-init
pattern in beforeAll (lines 55-71) is intentional for force-fresh isolation
+ the immediate initAsync at line 71 leaves the singleton properly initialized
for downstream specs. The `_initPromise = null` + `db = null` calls there
are safe because they're paired with re-init.

Server.spec.beforeEach also destroys (lines 50-58) but each test that runs
calls initAsync internally — leave those alone; only fix afterAll.

Iteration cost retro: should have applied Path B to all 4 specs in c.2e23dcaf7
instead of just FileSystemIngestor. The grep audit was the right empirical
verification step — should have run it BEFORE pushing the partial fix."
